rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative 1,2-difunctionalization of alkenes (styrenes) with formyloxylation coupled to chlorination, bromination, or trifluoromethylation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the halide/sulfinate anion (Br-, Cl-, or CF3SO2- giving CF3•) to a halogen/CF3 radical, which adds to the alkene; Cu(I) co-catalyst is also redox-active

key_intermediates.name: halogen or CF3 radical (X• or CF3•) from anodic oxidation of X- / CF3SO2-
benzylic carbon radical from X•/CF3• addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation after further oxidation of C-radical
formate (HCOO-) nucleophile trapping the benzylic cation to give formyloxy product

electron_flow: Halide or trifluoromethanesulfinate is first oxidized at the anode to X• (or CF3•), which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic C-radical; this radical is further oxidized (anode/Cu) to a benzylic cation that is trapped by formate (from HCOOH/H2O) to install the OCHO group, while protons are reduced at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: radical trapping experiments (see SI Scheme 2)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed/intercepted, supporting involvement of radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Indicates Br-, Cl-, or CF3SO2- (without Cu(I)) is oxidized first at the anode

notes: Diastereoselective trans-addition on cyclic alkenes suggests a stepwise radical/cation mechanism rather than concerted. Minor β-chlorinated side products from electron-rich styrenes are attributed to over-oxidation/elimination stabilized by push-pull conjugation. Detailed mechanism schemes are in the Supporting Information (Scheme 3).
